      have h₄ : ContinuousOn (fun t : ℝ => |∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, g'' s|) (Set.Icc 0 1) := by
        -- The function is continuous on [0,1]
        have h₅ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => |∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, g'' s|) := by
          -- The function is continuous
          apply Continuous.abs
          apply Continuous.intervalIntegral_comp_right
          <;> (try norm_num)
          <;> (try exact g''_cont)
          <;> (try continuity)
          <;> (try
            {
              apply Continuous.intervalIntegrable
              <;>
              (try continuity)
              <;>
              (try simp_all [g''_cont])
              <;>
              (try norm_num)
            })
          <;> (try
            {
              norm_num
            })
          <;> (try
            {
              linarith
            })
        exact h₅.continuousOn
      -- Use the fact that the integral of the absolute value is greater than or equal to the absolute value of the integral
      have h₅ : ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..1, |∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, g'' s| ≤ ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..1, ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, |g'' s| := by
        -- Apply the integral comparison
        calc
          ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..1, |∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, g'' s| ≤ ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..1, ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, |g'' s| := by
            -- Use the fact that the integral of the absolute value is greater than or equal to the absolute value of the integral
            have h₆ : ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1, |∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, g'' s| ≤ ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, |g'' s| := by
              intro t ht
              have h₇ : 0 ≤ t := by
                exact ht.1
              exact abs_int_le_int_abs h₇
            -- Use the fact that the integral of the absolute value is greater than or equal to the absolute value of the integral
            exact intervalIntegral.integral_mono_on (by
              -- The function is continuous on [0,1]
              apply Continuous.intervalIntegrable
              <;>
              (try continuity)
              <;>
              (try simp_all [g''_cont])
              <;>
              (try norm_num)) (by
              -- The function is continuous on [0,1]
              apply Continuous.intervalIntegrable
              <;>
              (try continuity)
              <;>
              (try simp_all [g''_cont])
              <;>
              (try norm_num)) (by
              -- The function is continuous on [0,1]
              intro x hx
              exact h₆ x ⟨by linarith [hx.1, hx.2], by linarith [hx.1, hx.2]⟩) (by
              -- The interval is [0,1]
              norm_num)
          _ = ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..1, ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, |g'' s| := by rfl
      exact h₅
    exact h₂
  
  sorry

have h₃ : ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1, |∫ (s : ℝ) in (0 : ℝ)..t, g'' s| ≤ ∫ (s : ℝ) in (0 : ℝ)..t, |g'' s| := by
      intro t ht
      have h₄ : 0 ≤ t := by exact ht.1
      have h₅ : |∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, g'' s| ≤ ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, |g'' s| := abs_int_le_int_abs h₄
      simpa using h₅
    have h₄ : ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..1, |∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, g'' s| ≤ ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..1, ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, |g'' s| := by
      calc
        _ = ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..1, |∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, g'' s| := rfl
        _ ≤ ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..1, ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, |g'' s| := by
          -- Use the fact that the integrand on the LHS is less than or equal to the integrand on the RHS
          refine' intervalIntegral.integral_mono_on (by norm_num) (by norm_num) _
          intro t ht
          have h₅ : t ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := by
            constructor <;> linarith [ht.1, ht.2]
          exact h₃ t h₅
        _ = ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..1, ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, |g'' s| := rfl
    exact h₄
  exact int_abs_int_le_int_int_abs

have h₁ : (∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..1, |∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, g'' s|) ≤ ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..1, ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, |g'' s| := by
      have h₂ : ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1, |∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, g'' s| ≤ ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, |g'' s| := by
        intro t ht
        have ht' : 0 ≤ t := by
          simp [Set.mem_Icc] at ht
          linarith
        exact abs_int_le_int_abs ht'
      have h₃ : ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..1, |∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, g'' s| ≤ ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..1, ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, |g'' s| := by
        -- Use the fact that the integral of a smaller function is smaller
        have h₄ : ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..1, |∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, g'' s| ≤ ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..1, ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, |g'' s| := by
          -- Use the fact that the integral of a smaller function is smaller
          refine' intervalIntegral.integral_mono_on (by continuity) (by
            have h₅ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, |g'' s| := by
              sorry
            continuity) (Set.Icc 0 1) _
          intro t ht
          exact h₂ t ht
        exact h₄
      exact h₃
    exact h₁

  sorry

apply intervalIntegral.integral_mono_on (by norm_num) (by norm_num) (fun t ht => ?_)
      · -- Prove that the function is integrable
        refine' Continuous.intervalIntegrable _ 0 1 _

            have h₂ : Continuous g'' := by assumption
            have h₃ : Continuous (fun (t : ℝ) => ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, g'' s) := by

        refine' Continuous.intervalIntegrable _ 0 1 _
        -- Prove that the double integral is continuous
        have h₁ : Continuous (fun (t : ℝ) => ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, |g'' s|) := by
          -- Use the fact that |g'' s| is continuous and the integral of a continuous function is continuous
          have h₂ : Continuous (fun s : ℝ => |g'' s|) := by

          have h₃ : Continuous (fun (t : ℝ) => ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, |g'' s|) := by

            <;> try continuity

        exact h₁
      · -- Prove that |∫ g'' s| ≤ ∫ |g'' s|
        intro t ht
        have h₁ : |∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, g'' s| ≤ ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, |g'' s| := by

have h₁ : ∀ (t : ℝ), 0 ≤ t → |∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, g'' s| ≤ ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, |g'' s| := abs_int_le_int_abs
    have h₂ : (∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..1, |∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, g'' s| ) ≤ ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..1, ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, |g'' s| := by

            -- Prove that the inner integral is continuous

        ) (Set.Icc 0 1) (by

have h₁ : ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1, |∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, g'' s| ≤ ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, |g'' s| := by
      intro t ht
      have h₂ : 0 ≤ t := by exact ht.1
      have h₃ : t ≤ 1 := by exact ht.2
      exact abs_int_le_int_abs h₂
    -- Apply the monotonicity of integration
    have h₂ : ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..1, |∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, g'' s| ≤ ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..1, ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, |g'' s| := by
      apply intervalIntegral.integral_mono_on (by continuity) (by
        -- Show that the integral of |g''| is continuous
        have h₃ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, |g'' s|) := by
          -- Since g'' is continuous, |g''| is continuous, and thus the integral is continuous
          have h₄ : Continuous g'' := by sorry
          have h₅ : Continuous (fun s : ℝ => |g'' s|) := by sorry
          -- Use the fact that the integral of a continuous function is continuous
          apply Continuous.intervalIntegral_comp_continuous
          <;> continuity
        continuity
      ) (Set.Icc 0 1)
      -- Use the fact that for each t, |∫ g''| ≤ ∫ |g''|
      intro t ht
      exact h₁ t ht
    exact h₂
